stonetop.summary: The trickster fox-spirit — charming and thieving, bound to its old skull.

system.concept: The trickster fox-spirit — charming and thieving, bound to its old skull.
system.description: Every child has heard stories of the fox-spirit, the trickster, the fadyn. In some tales, a fadyn makes fools of the cruel or the proud. In others, they steal chickens from the slow-witted. And in still others, they seduce a beautiful human, disappearing when bored or discovered. Truth be told, there's little pattern to who a fadyn torments or fancies. They are fickle, fickle things.

lore.prompt: **Something interesting:** When a fadyn takes the form of a person, it always has some fox-like feature (a tail, feet, hands, ears) that it strives to hide. It is easily embarrassed by this feature, and flees if it's discovered.
**Something useful:** Fadyn are the spirits of old, cagey foxes who passed away, tethered to their old skulls. One can't travel far from its skull — get hold of it to destroy or bind the fadyn.
